payload.text:
DUKAANMITRA MVP BUILD SPECIFICATION (DEC 2025)
SECTION 1: SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

End-to-End Flow:

User Action: Customer or Shop Owner sends a WhatsApp message to the Gupshup Business number.

Ingress: Gupshup captures the message and triggers an HTTP POST Webhook.

Orchestration (Make.com):

Node A (Webhook): Receives raw JSON payload.

Node B (Router/Filter): Checks if the sender's phone number exists in Google Sheets.

Node C (AI Parsing): If transaction-related, the AI module extracts "Amount," "Items," and "Name."

Storage (Google Sheets):

Logs customer info (if new).

Records transaction entry in the Ledger sheet.

Egress: Make.com calls Gupshup API to send a template-based confirmation message back to the user.

Billing: Razorpay triggers a separate webhook on payment success, updating the 'PaidStatus' in Google Sheets.

SECTION 2: ACCOUNT SETUP LINKS

Make.com: https://www.make.com/en/register

Gupshup WhatsApp API: https://www.gupshup.io/developer/whatsapp-api

Google Sheets: https://sheets.google.com

Razorpay Dashboard: https://dashboard.razorpay.com

SECTION 3: GOOGLE SHEETS STRUCTURE
Sheet 1: Customers
Column	Name	Data Type	Default Value	Formula/Notes
A	Phone	String (Key)	-	Include country code (91)
B	Name	String	-	Extracted from WA profile
C	JoinDate	Date/Time	{{now}}	Set on first message
D	TrialEndDate	Date	-	Formula: =C2+30
E	PaidStatus	String	"Trial"	Values: Trial, Active, Expired
F	TotalLTV	Currency	0	Sum of Transactions
Sheet 2: Transactions
Column	Name	Data Type	Formula/Notes
A	TransID	UUID	Unique ID for reconciliation
B	Phone	String	Reference to Customers Sheet
C	Amount	Number	Extracted by AI Parsing
D	Items	String	Raw text summary of items bought
E	Date	Date/Time	Generated at timestamp of entry
Sheet 3: SystemLogs
Column	Name	Data Type	Purpose
A	Timestamp	Date/Time	Tracking error time
B	ErrorSource	String	e.g., AI Parser, Gupshup API
C	PayloadReceived	JSON	The exact JSON that failed
D	Resolution	String	Handled / Needs Intervention
SECTION 4: MAKE.COM SCENARIOS
Scenario 1: Main Inbound Message Handling

Trigger: Custom Webhook (Gupshup Webhook URL).

Module 2: Google Sheets - Search Rows (Find customer by Phone).

Module 3 (Router):

Path A (New User): If Phone not found -> Google Sheets - Add a Row (Customers).

Path B (Existing User): Continue to Parsing.

Module 4: Make.com AI Assistant/OpenAI

Purpose: Extract Name, Amount, and Items from {{message.text}}.

Module 5: Google Sheets - Add a Row (Transactions).

Module 6: Gupshup - Send a Message (Confirmation with transaction details).

Scenario 2: Trial Expiry Checker (Daily)

Trigger: Schedule (Every morning 09:00 IST).

Module 2: Google Sheets - Search Rows (Find TrialEndDate = today + 3 days).

Module 3: Gupshup - Send a Message (Template: Trial_Ending_Reminder).

Scenario 3: Razorpay Webhook (Payment Sync)

Trigger: Custom Webhook (Razorpay subscription.charged).

Module 2: Google Sheets - Search Rows (Match email/phone from Razorpay to Customers).

Module 3: Google Sheets - Update a Row (Change PaidStatus to "Active").

SECTION 5: WEBHOOK PAYLOADS
Incoming WhatsApp Message (Gupshup)
code
JSON
download
content_copy
expand_less
{
  "source": "919876543210",
  "payload": {
    "text": "Ram ne 2kg cheeni 150 rupaye ki li",
    "type": "text"
  },
  "sender": {
    "name": "Amit Sharma"
  }
}
AI Parsed Bill Output
code
JSON
download
content_copy
expand_less
{
  "customer_name": "Ram",
  "amount": 150,
  "items": "2kg cheeni",
  "confidence_score": 0.98
}
Razorpay Success
code
JSON
download
content_copy
expand_less
{
  "event": "subscription.charged",
  "payload": {
    "subscription": {
      "entity": {
        "notes": {
          "phone": "919876543210"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
SECTION 6: WHATSAPP MESSAGE TEMPLATES

1. Welcome Message (welcome_msg):
"Namaste {{1}}! DukaanMitra CRM me aapka swagat hai. Aaj se aapke sabhi hisaab yahan safe rahenge. Message format: 'Aman 500 doodh cheeni'."

2. Bill Confirmation (bill_conf):
"Bil Update:
Customer: {{1}}
Amount: ₹{{2}}
Items: {{3}}
Hisaab confirm ho gaya hai! ✅"

3. Format Error (format_error):
"Maaf kijiye, humein samajh nahi aaya. Kripya is tarah likhein: 'Naam Amount Samaan'. Example: 'Rahul 100 chai'."

SECTION 7: FAILURE HANDLING

Webhook Failure: If Make.com returns 4xx/5xx, Gupshup automatically retries 3 times. Errors are logged in SystemLogs via a Make.com Error Handler route on the webhook node.

Duplicate Detection: Phone Number is the primary key. Before adding a new customer, "Search Row" checks for existing Phone records to prevent duplicate billing profiles.

AI Parsing Failure: If the AI module returns a confidence score < 0.7, the system bypasses the Transaction log and sends the Format Error template to the user.

Kill-Switch: Manual override column in Google Sheets (SystemLogs) "Pause_Bot" set to TRUE will prevent any messages from being sent to the user in case of an infinite loop error.

SECTION 8: MVP LIMITATIONS

No Multi-Admin Support: Only one Gupshup number maps to one spreadsheet; scaling to thousands of shops requires one sheet per shop.

Concurrent Messaging: Google Sheets has an API limit (approx. 60 requests/minute). For >100 transactions/min, system must migrate to PostgreSQL/Supabase.

Attachment Handling: MVP does not parse images of handwritten bills. Text only.

Inventory Tracking: The MVP tracks cash flow and customer names, but does not deduct stock levels from an inventory database.

SECTION 9: DAY 1 – DAY 7 BUILD PLAN

Day 1: Connection Stack. Set up Gupshup Sandbox, Make.com account, and link them to a single Google Sheet. Verify Webhook handshake.

Day 2: Core Ledger Flow. Build Scenario 1 (Inbound msg). Implement basic parsing logic (Text-to-Sheet).

Day 3: AI Intelligence. Connect AI modules in Make.com for Hinglish parsing. Test with varied Kirana shop vocabulary.

Day 4: Confirmation Loop. Create and approve WhatsApp Templates. Ensure response messages are triggering within 5 seconds of request.

Day 5: Customer State Management. Implement New User vs Returning User detection. Add Trial Expiry calculation logic.

Day 6: Payment Integration. Setup Razorpay Subscription links and link webhook to Google Sheets for "Active" status updates.

Day 7: Beta Testing & Fail-safes. Stress test with 100 simultaneous messages. Review SystemLogs for timeout or parsing errors.

END SPECIFICATION.

**Building the MVP Dashboard**

I'm currently focused on the React application that will serve as the admin dashboard for DukaanMitra. It's envisioned as an MVP visualizer, and I'm prioritizing the integration with WhatsApp via Gupshup, as well as considering orchestration through Make.com and Google Sheets for data storage. My key concern is to design a user-friendly interface for managing the ledger data.


**Expanding the Simulator Scope**

I'm now expanding the simulator's capabilities. I'm building a more robust mock WhatsApp interface that integrates with Gemini for simulating real-time interactions, including Hinglish text. This will drive the dashboard's visualization of customer and transaction data from the mocked "Google Sheets". I've created the basic structure: routing and state in `App.tsx`, tables to visualize the "data" in `CustomerList.tsx` and `TransactionList.tsx`, and charts to display the "scenario data" in `Dashboard.tsx`. I'm starting work on the chat interface.


**Designing the UI and Gemini Integration**

My focus is now on the UI and integrating Gemini. I'm building a system logs table in `SystemLogs.tsx` to visualize AI-parsed transactions. Gemini will parse Hinglish messages into structured JSON via `geminiService.ts`. I want a clean, professional, responsive, dark/light mode SaaS look. I'm using `constants.ts` for mock data and configs, and `types.ts` for interfaces. The AI will receive Hinglish strings as input, and output structured JSON. We're on the path.


**Implementing Charting and Components**

I'm now integrating charting libraries, specifically Recharts, to visualize "Total LTV" and "Transactions per day". I'm also incorporating Lucide React icons via CDN for visual enhancements. Components like `TransactionList.tsx`, `Simulator.tsx`, and `SystemLogs.tsx` are in progress, and I'm using the `gemini-3-flash-preview` model for parsing with the Gemini service. My goal is to add some interactivity!

entries.description: Service to interact with Gemini API for Hinglish parsing
Dashboard overview with stats and charts

diffs.replacement:
import React from 'react'; import { BarChart, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer, LineChart, Line } from 'recharts'; import { Customer, Transaction } from '../types'; interface DashboardProps { customers: Customer[]; transactions: Transaction[]; } const Dashboard: React.FC = ({ customers, transactions }) => { const stats = [ { label: 'Total Customers', value: customers.length, icon: '👥', color: 'bg-blue-500' }, { label: 'Total Revenue', value: `₹${customers.reduce((acc, c) => acc + c.totalLTV, 0)}`, icon: '💰', color: 'bg-emerald-500' }, { label: 'Trial Users', value: customers.filter(c => c.paidStatus === 'Trial').length, icon: '⏳', color: 'bg-amber-500' }, { label: 'Transactions (MTD)', value: transactions.length, icon: '📝', color: 'bg-purple-500' }, ]; // Prepare chart data (Last 7 days of transactions) const chartData = [ { name: 'Mon', count: 4, revenue: 1200 }, { name: 'Tue', count: 7, revenue: 2100 }, { name: 'Wed', count: 5, revenue: 1500 }, { name: 'Thu', count: 8, revenue: 2800 }, { name: 'Fri', count: 12, revenue: 4500 }, { name: 'Sat', count: 15, revenue: 5200 }, { name: 'Sun', count: 10, revenue: 3800 }, ]; return (

Operational Overview

Real-time stats from the DukaanMitra Ledger Engine.

{/* Stats Grid */}
{stats.map((s, idx) => (
{s.icon}

{s.label}

{s.value}

))}
{/* Revenue Chart */}

Daily Revenue Trends

{/* Transaction Volume */}

Transaction Volume

{/* Trial Expiry Warning Section */}
⏳

Upcoming Trial Expiries

{customers.filter(c => c.paidStatus === 'Trial').map((c, i) => (

{c.name}

Expires: {new Date(c.trialEndDate).toLocaleDateString()}

3 Days Left
))}
); }; export default Dashboard;

entries.description: Main App component with view state and layout

payload.text:
ROLE:
You are a senior deployment engineer and no-code systems integrator.

You already have a Build Specification for a product called “DukaanMitra”.
Now your task is to EXECUTE Phase 2: Deployment.

This is NOT design.
This is NOT architecture.
This is LIVE SYSTEM EXECUTION GUIDANCE.

========================
PHASE 2 OBJECTIVE
========================

Transform the approved Build Specification into a WORKING, LIVE MVP
using Google Sheets, Gupshup, Make.com, and Razorpay.

Assume:
- Phase 1 (design & build spec) is approved
- This guide will be followed step-by-step by a founder
- Zero code environment
- India-first WhatsApp deployment

========================
MODULE 1: DATABASE CONSTRUCTION (GOOGLE SHEETS)
========================

Provide EXACT steps to create the production database.

Create File:
- Name: DukaanMitra_Prod_v1

Sheet: Customers
- Column A: Phone → format = Plain Text (prevent truncation)
- Column B: Name → Text
- Column C: JoinDate → Date Time
- Column D: TrialEndDate → Formula
- Column E: PaidStatus → Default = TRIAL
- Column F: LastActivity → Date Time

Formula:
- Apply to D2:
=IF(C2<>"", C2+30, "")
- Drag formula down to row 1000

Sheet: Transactions
- Column A: Date
- Column B: Phone
- Column C: Amount
- Column D: Items
- Column E: Source

Optional:
- Apply Data Validation dropdown on Transactions!B:B
  Source: Customers!A:A

Permissions:
- Share → Anyone with link → Editor
- Explain WHY this is required for Make.com stability

========================
MODULE 2: GUPSHUP CONNECTIVITY
========================

Explain step-by-step:

Sandbox Setup:
- Go to Gupshup Console
- WhatsApp Dashboard → Create App → Service
- Smart Mechanisms → Opt-in → Disable (Sandbox only)

Coexistence API (Critical):
- Settings → Phone Numbers → Import Number
- Explain OBO (On-Behalf-Of) flow
- Emphasize: No chat history loss
- Mention when Meta approval is required vs not

========================
MODULE 3: MAKE.COM LOGIC (INBOUND SCENARIO)
========================

Scenario Name:
Incoming_WhatsApp_Handler

Step 1: Webhook Inbound
- Module: Webhooks → Custom Webhook
- Create new → Copy URL
- Paste into Gupshup Callback URL
- Test message: “Test 100”
- Explain how to confirm payload arrival

Step 2: AI Parse (Engine)
- Module: Make AI or OpenAI → Extract Data
- Model: gpt-4o-mini
- Prompt:

Analyze this WhatsApp message: "{{webhook_text}}"
Sender Name: {{webhook_sender_name}}

Identify:
1. Action (BILLING or GENERAL_QUERY)
2. Amount (Integer only)
3. Items (List)
4. Name of debtor (else null)

Return ONLY valid JSON.

Step 3: State Router
- Search Customers Sheet by Phone
- Route A: Phone NOT FOUND → Add Row (Customers)
- Route B: AI_Action == BILLING → Add Row (Transactions)

Explain filters clearly.

========================
MODULE 4: RAZORPAY SUBSCRIPTION LOGIC
========================

Plan Creation:
- Name: DukaanMitra_Basic
- Amount: ₹499
- Period: Monthly

Webhook:
- Create new Make.com webhook
- Events:
  - subscription.authenticated
  - payment.captured

Scenario Logic:
- Extract phone from Razorpay metadata (notes.phone)
- Update Customers sheet:
  PaidStatus = ACTIVE

Warn about:
- Missing phone metadata = broken automation

========================
MODULE 5: WHATSAPP TEMPLATE APPROVALS (GUPSHUP)
========================

Provide templates ready for Meta approval:

Template ID: dm_welcome
Language: Hindi
Category: Utility

Message:
Namaste! Swagat hai DukaanMitra mein.
Aapka phone number register ho chuka hai.
30 din ka free trial shuru!
Pehla bill likhein:
"Rahul 250 Dudh Dahi"

Template ID: dm_bill_success
Category: Utility

Message:
Entry Confirm! ✅
Naam: {{1}}
Amount: {{2}}
Item: {{3}}

========================
MODULE 6: FAILURE HANDLER ROUTE
========================

Attach Error Handler to AI Parse module.

Condition:
- If Confidence < 0.7 OR required fields missing

Action:
- Send WhatsApp message:
"Maaf kijiye, humein bill samajh nahi aaya.
Kripya 'Naam Amount Item' format mein likhein."

Explain why graceful failure is critical.

========================
DATA INTEGRITY CHECKLIST
========================

Before deployment, verify:
- Country code consistency (+91)
- Google Sheet timezone = IST
- Make.com scenario = Immediate
- Gupshup wallet balance ≥ ₹500

========================
SCALING: 1 → 20 PILOTS
========================

Explain multi-tenant handling:

- Add column: StoreID in Customers sheet
- Map incoming WhatsApp number to StoreID
- Ensure data segregation per shop

========================
FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Write this as a DEPLOYMENT EXECUTION MANUAL.
Clear steps.
No fluff.
No assumptions.
No invented APIs.
Flag anything requiring human confirmation.

Begin Phase 2 output now.

**Charting the Deployment Course**

I'm focused on the practical aspects of rolling out DukaanMitra Phase 2. The Build Spec is in hand, and I'm now strategizing the deployment steps. My priority is seamless integration, making sure the WhatsApp-based ledger system functions flawlessly. This involves detailed planning and troubleshooting potential hurdles before they appear.


**Constructing Deployment Portal**

Now I'm designing a new "Deployment Guide" view within the React app. This will function as an in-app portal for the manual. Adding a deployment portal fulfills the human-centric guide requirement by making the app its own deployment manual, ensuring a smoother rollout. I'll integrate step-by-step instructions directly into the application.


**Implementing the Checklist Guide**

I'm now implementing the interactive checklist view within the app to guide users through the deployment. This entails building a new `DeploymentGuide.tsx` component, integrating navigation, and handling the application's state. I'm focusing on providing clear, step-by-step instructions with code snippets and copy-pasteable elements. The aesthetics will align with the existing sleek dashboard, using Tailwind for consistent styling.


**Deciding the Response Type**

I'm wrestling with how to interpret the user's latest request. Their prompt seems to combine a request for a document with a demand for app updates. Initially, I was leaning towards generating the deployment guide. Now, I'm thinking of focusing on providing the necessary code changes as an immediate response. I'll need to update the `metadata.json`, `types.ts`, `Layout.tsx`, and `App.tsx` files and add a new `DeploymentGuide.tsx` component. The user's instructions regarding an "AI prompt" seem more relevant to crafting the manual's content.


**Initiating Code Adjustments**

I've interpreted the user's latest input as a directive to update the app's core code. The user gave me the content they want the app to use and become. I'm focusing on updating `types.ts`, `components/Layout.tsx`, and will create `components/DeploymentGuide.tsx`. I will also address `App.tsx`. The goal is to build the "Deployment Portal" as specified.

### Specification: DukaanMitra Deployment Portal Update

The application will be transformed from a simple monitoring dashboard into a **Unified Deployment & Management Portal**. This update integrates the "Phase 2: Deployment Execution Manual" directly into the user interface, providing shop owners and system integrators with a step-by-step interactive checklist to go live.

#### 1. UI/UX Enhancements
- **New Navigation Item**: "Deployment Guide" added to the sidebar with a 🚀 icon.
- **Interactive Guide**: A multi-step stepper component that breaks down the deployment into the 6 modules defined in the specification (Database, Gupshup, Make.com, Razorpay, Templates, Failure Handling).
- **Copy-to-Clipboard Utility**: Every code snippet (Google Sheet formulas, AI Prompts, JSON payloads) will feature a "Copy" button for zero-friction execution.
- **Visual Checklists**: Each module will have a persistent checklist state (local session) so builders can track their progress.

#### 2. Functional Updates
- **Type System Expansion**: Added `deployment` view to the routing logic.
- **Dynamic Content Rendering**: The guide will render the exact Make.com prompts and Google Sheet structures provided in the Deployment Execution Manual.
- **Live Simulator Integration**: The existing Simulator will be cross-linked within the guide to allow "Dry Run" testing of the AI logic before actual Make.com deployment.

#### 3. Visual Appearance
- **Clean Documentation Style**: Uses a "Documentation-First" aesthetic with clear typography, breadcrumbs, and callout boxes (Warning, Pro-Tip, Critical).
- **Interactive Snippets**: Code blocks will use a dark syntax theme (slate-900) to contrast against the light dashboard UI.
- **Progressive Disclosure**: Detailed steps are organized in collapsible cards to prevent information overload.

---

entries.description: Update sidebar with Deployment Guide link
New interactive deployment execution manual component
